name: repo-orchestrator
description: Use this agent when the user needs to analyze, document, or review a large repository that cannot fit in a single context window. This includes tasks like generating architecture documentation, performing codebase-wide code review, identifying architectural drift, refreshing stale documentation, or understanding component relationships across a monorepo. The orchestrator should be the entry point for any large-scale repo analysis effort.\\n\\nExamples:\\n\\n- User: \"I need to understand the architecture of this entire monorepo and generate documentation for it.\"\\n  Assistant: \"This is a large-scale repository analysis task. Let me use the Agent tool to launch the repo-orchestrator agent to decompose the repository into components and coordinate the analysis.\"\\n\\n- User: \"Can you do a full code review of this codebase and identify architectural issues?\"\\n  Assistant: \"A codebase-wide review requires systematic decomposition. Let me use the Agent tool to launch the repo-orchestrator agent to plan the analysis, assign bounded worker tasks, and synthesize findings.\"\\n\\n- User: \"Our documentation is completely out of date. Can you refresh it across the whole repo?\"\\n  Assistant: \"Refreshing documentation across a large repo requires mapping components first, then generating docs systematically. Let me use the Agent tool to launch the repo-orchestrator agent to coordinate this multi-phase effort.\"\\n\\n- User: \"I want to find all the places where we have dependency direction violations or circular dependencies.\"\\n  Assistant: \"Cross-cutting architectural analysis requires component-level evidence first. Let me use the Agent tool to launch the repo-orchestrator agent to build the component graph and schedule targeted analysis passes.\"
